_meta.notes: Dwarven homeland. Terraced highland orchards above the cloudline. Dwarves cannot tolerate enclosed spaces; every dwelling is open-air or open-roofed; many sleep on porches that face the cloud-mass. Whitebark cider is the city's signature export; cloudtea is the local breakfast drink. A Gust-watcher's clearance is required before any planting or harvest.

tagline: Above the clouds. Wide skies, narrow paths, no roofs.
ambientPool: Wind moves continuously across the terraces; even sheltered spots have a whisper to them.
Cloud cover is below you. Looking down is more disorienting than looking up.
A pollination kite passes overhead — a Skybeam keeping bees moving between blocks.
Two Gust-watchers stand in their separate clearings and read the wind by feel.
The air is thin enough that strangers from below breathe more often than the dwarves do.

rooms.summary: The arrival terrace. Long stone bench facing outward over the cloud-mass. New arrivals from below sit here to acclimate. Nobody hurries them.
ambientPool: A Topgrove orchardist watches a stranger catch their breath and offers a cup of cloudtea without comment.
The wind shifts; a Gust-watcher across two terraces nods at the change.
An apple drops from a heritage tree two terraces up and is caught by a child waiting for exactly that.
A Skybeam threads a kite line through a pulley overhead, smoothly.

exits.narrative: Up the stone steps onto the orchard terraces.
Sideways and up, toward the open-roofed cider works (no enclosed cellars here, despite the name).
Up further to the Gust-watchers' clearing — the highest open ground.

rooms.summary: Stepped orchards on stone steppes. Each terrace block holds one tree species. Whitestar apples on the lowest, Greybark pears in the middle, Sun-cherries at the top.
ambientPool: A Topgrove of the Whitestar line walks slowly along the terrace edge, looking at every fourth tree.
Skybeam kites work the air between blocks; a bee-cluster releases at exactly the moment the wind asks it to.
An apprentice ties a sapling to a stake with a knot that takes longer than the speech they are giving while doing it.
Far below, a cloud parts; a halfling sail can be seen on the coast for half a second before the cloud closes.

exits.narrative: Down the stone steps to the arrival terrace.
Across and up to the cider works.
Continuing upward to the highest clearing.

rooms.summary: Whitebark cider works. Misnamed — none of it is underground; the casks sit on open-roofed stone platforms. A canopy of woven branches keeps the rain off; the wind is welcome.
ambientPool: A Whitebark master taps a birch cask and nods at the sound it makes.
Two Whitebarks taste a new batch and disagree, courteously, in dwarven wind-pace.
A Topgrove who has come to deliver Greybark pears stays for three cups of cider and a long conversation about whether the season is early.
The wind catches a cask-cover and the master adjusts it without breaking conversation.

rooms.summary: The highest open ground in the city. Bone weather instruments arranged on a stone platform; a single Gust-watcher sits cross-legged in the center.
ambientPool: The wind is loud enough here that the Gust-watcher uses small bone instruments instead of speech.
A second Gust-watcher arrives, sits, and starts reading.
A Topgrove climbs up to ask a question and gets the answer in three gestures.
Below, the orchard terraces are barely visible through cloud.
